question_text: Which of the following statements best explains why a piece of cork floats on water?

options.label: A
options.text: Cork is made of a lighter material than water

options.label: B
options.text: Cork has a lower density than water, so it floats

options.label: C
options.text: Cork floats because it is less heavy than water

options.label: D
options.text: Cork floats because water pushes it upwards

explanation: Objects float when their density is lower than the fluid they're in. Cork floats on water because cork's density (about 0.24 g/cm³) is less than water's density (1 g/cm³). Option B correctly applies the density concept to explain floating.
